news.title: U.S. strikes Iran after Trump accuses Tehran of ceasefire violation in Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: The attack comes as the United States and Iran are supposed to be engaging in a 60-period of no hostilities as they hold talks to end their war.

news.title: China's Zhipu is closing in on top U.S. AI models with Anthropic and OpenAI held back
news.summary: Zhipu's GLM 5.2 shows the AI fight is shifting to who delivers the most intelligence per dollar, making open source suddenly a real contender.

news.title: Markets, Economy Ignoring Policy Shocks: Skelly
news.summary: Dan Skelly, managing director and head of the equity model portfolio team at Morgan Stanley Wealth Management, joins Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Dow Jones Finishes Day Down; Week Up | Closing Bell
news.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Coffee Company Kahawa 1893 Works to Empower Women Farmers
news.summary: Margaret Nyambumo, founder of coffee company Kahawa 1893 and third-generation coffee farmer, said that she created the option to directly tip the female farmers who grow her coffee beans because she wanted to find a way to consistently and fairly compensate them regardless of fluctuation in coffee prices. Nyambumo said that the tips from customers — which are matched by the company — have been used by one of the farming collectives to start a scholarship for young girls to stay in school. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Education 'Getting Act Together' on Personal Finance: Cohen
news.summary: Abby Joseph Cohen, professor at Columbia Business School, joins Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss personal finance and the US education system's effort to educate students on financial literacy. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Alternative Soda Brands Taking Over Beverage Markets
news.summary: Poppi Co-Founder Allison Ellsworth describes struggles of breaking into beverage industry, warns getting product on shelves is "capital intensive." Ellsworth says movement within "MAHA" is changing industry standards, including for beverage giants like Pepsi which purchased Poppi in 2025 for nearly $2 Billion. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Abby Joseph Cohen Talks Greenspan's Legacy
news.summary: Abby Joseph Cohen, professor at Columbia Business School, joins Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss the legacy of former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan who died this week at the age of 100. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Look Ahead: US Jobs Report, America 250
news.summary: Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene discuss the week ahead for the markets and your money on this weeks edition of "Bloomberg Money." (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Micron sinks 6%, wrapping a wild week of trading that saw big swings
news.summary: Micron Technology's shares tumbled on Friday amid a global sell-off in chip stocks.

news.title: Walmart heir Lukas Walton buys minority stake in the Chicago Bulls and United Center
news.summary: Lukas Walton is the 39-year-old grandson of Walmart founder Sam Walton. He and his wife, Samantha, are residents of Chicago.

news.title: Carl Richards on the Power of Compounding
news.summary: Barry sits down with Carl Richards author of "Your Money: Reimagining Wealth in Simple Sketches ." They discuss Carl's unlikely start in finance and building his own firm. Carl also breaks down how one sketch helped him translate wealth management and become a New York Times columnist. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Sketching Wealth Strategy: Masters in Business with Carl Richards
news.summary: Barry sits down with Carl Richards author of "Your Money: Reimagining Wealth in Simple Sketches ". They discuss Carl's unlikely start in finance and building his own firm. Carl also breaks down how one sketch helped him translate wealth management and become a New York Times columnist. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: キオクシア米上場計画、高市政権「370兆円投資」、投資のプロ4人が明かす1000万円投資術──1週間のニュース5選
news.summary: マーケットで話題になったニュースから、1週間を振り返る五つのトピックを厳選して紹介します。

news.title: Must-Read Books About Alan Greenspan's Legacy
news.summary: Tom Keene and Lisa Mateo discuses the must-read books about Alan Greenspan's legacy on this weeks edition of "Bloomberg Money." Former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan died this week at the age of 100. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Gavin Newsom calls for national billionaires tax: 'It's time for an economic reset'
news.summary: California Gov. Gavin Newsom said he supports imposing "a true minimum tax on billionaires" and closing "tax-free lifestyle loan" loopholes for the rich.

news.title: Oracle stock has worst week since 2001 dot-com bust as AI financing concerns escalate
news.summary: Oracle's surging spending, negative free cash flow and $130 billion debt pile are weighing on the stock.

news.title: AI Rout Exposes Wall Street’s $270 Billion Speculation Machine
news.summary: This week’s tech rout did more than puncture one of the year’s hottest trades: it exposed the machinery of modern speculation — and how quickly it can work in reverse.

news.title: Bloomberg Money 6/26/2026
news.summary: Bloomberg Money takes the pulse of your financial life, powered by the reporting of our global newsroom. Today's guests are: Columbia Business School Professor Abby Joseph Cohen and Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Managing Director Daniel Skelly. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: ON Semiconductor records worst day since 2020 as CEO defends Synaptics deal
news.summary: The pivot into physical AI grows On Semi's addressable market by an additional $30 billion, the company said.

news.title: Hubble Pushes Satellite Network For Long-Range Bluetooth
news.summary: Alex Haro Co-Founder & CEO at Hubble Network joins Bloomberg Businessweek to the future of Bluetooth and the space economy. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Magnolia Oil & Gas Is in Lead to Acquire WildFire for Over $4 Billion
news.summary: Magnolia Oil & Gas Corp. has emerged as the front-runner to acquire closely held WildFire Energy for more than $4 billion in what would rank as its largest-ever acquisition, according to people familiar with the matter.

news.title: Stock Valuations Should Worry Investors: Abby Joseph Cohen
news.summary: Abby Joseph Cohen, professor at Columbia Business School, joins Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Lofty stock prices may be hiding risks investors should be paying more attention to, especially in the labor market, according to Abby Joseph Cohen, the former Goldman Sachs partner and now Columbia Business School professor. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: TPG, Leonard Green Explore Sale of McIlroy-Backed Troon Golf
news.summary: The private equity owners of Troon Golf are exploring a potential sale of the company, which is one of the largest US golf course operators, according to people familiar with the matter.

news.title: CFTC Conducting Broad Probe Into Prediction Market Polymarket
news.summary: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is conducting an extensive investigation into Polymarket, according to a person familiar with the matter, the latest challenge for the prediction market platform plagued by operational stumbles and legal scrutiny.

news.title: Money Roundtable: Hidden Inflation, Social Security
news.summary: Bloomberg's David Gura and Romaine Bostick join Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss this week's US inflation report, Apple's price hike, and the future of social security. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: South Korean Regulators Are Lamenting Leveraged ETFs
news.summary: Frenzied trading in South Korea has renewed concerns about the leveraged ETF boom

news.title: John Bolton Pleads Guilty to Mishandling Classified Material
news.summary: John Bolton, a former national security adviser to President Donald Trump who became one of his fiercest critics, appeared in court on Friday to plead guilty to unlawfully retaining national defense information. Robert McWhirter, Constitutional lawyer and a practicing criminal defense and civil rights lawyer in Maricopa County, AZ and Founder of the Law Office of Robert McWhirter, joined to discuss the case as well as the recent decisions from the Supreme Court. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Venezuela Quake Crisis Tests Legitimacy of Rodriguez Regime
news.summary: The earthquakes that struck Venezuela Wednesday have become the first major political test for acting President Delcy Rodríguez, with the disaster quickly evolving into a contest over competence. (Source: Bloomberg)

news.title: Exxon’s Top US Gas Trader to Join Expand as Exits Hit Oil Giant
news.summary: Exxon Mobil Corp.’s head of US gas and power trading is moving to Expand Energy Corp., one of several recent departures from a unit the supermajor has been trying to grow.

news.title: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on countries putting 'Digital Services Tax on American Companies'
news.summary: "This TARIFF will supersede Trade Deals made with the Country, whether implemented, signed, or not," President Donald Trump wrote in a Truth Social post.

news.title: Healthcare stocks have caught fire, sending all 3 of our names to record highs
news.summary: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

news.title: Leon Black refuses to answer questions on NDAs at Jeffrey Epstein hearing, Rep. Comer says
news.summary: "I knew Jekyll. I didn't know Hyde," Leon Black says about Jeffrey Epstein in his prepared statement to the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee.

news.title: Rubio says Israel, Lebanon reach framework agreement aimed at 'lasting peace and security'
news.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to implement a ceasefire. But that is contingent on the paramilitary group Hezbollah agreeing to halt hostilities.

news.title: OpenAI is reportedly delaying its IPO. Here's when Kalshi traders think it will announce
news.summary: Speculators think the OpenAI IPO will come early next year, with only one-in-three odds it happens in 2026 but a high likelihood it is done by June 2027.

news.title: Imposter scams led fraud reports to the FTC for fifth straight year in 2025, causing $3.5 billion in losses
news.summary: The amount lost to imposter scams is part of $15.9 billion in total fraud losses reported to the Federal Trade Commission in 2025

news.title: Trump eases pressure on Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh as inflation tops 4%
news.summary: President Donald Trump's economic advisors are giving Warsh space, as the new Fed chairman, on interest rates as the president repeats calls to cut.

news.title: OpenAI hasn't held pre-IPO investor meetings or set timeline yet, sources say
news.summary: OpenAI said it confidentially filed its prospectus with the SEC earlier this month, but noted it "may be a while" before it goes public.

news.title: OpenAI limits new AI models to 'trusted partners' at request of U.S. government
news.summary: OpenAI said it previewed the new models' capabilities with the government ahead of the launch.

news.title: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari says he expects a rate hike this year
news.summary: Kashkari said he sees a hike likely this year as the economy continues to feel the hit from spiking inflation.

news.title: Ex-Trump advisor John Bolton pleads guilty to retaining national defense information
news.summary: John Bolton is one of several foes of President Donald Trump who has been indicted since Trump regained the White House.

news.title: Jeremy Grantham says this is the most expensive market in 'American history'
news.summary: Jeremy Grantham says soaring valuations around AI makes the U.S. stock market historically expensive.

news.title: OpenAI and Anthropic face new AI reality as users shift from 'tokenmaxxing' to efficiency
news.summary: Companies are tightening their AI budgets to focus on getting a return on their investment, and that could dampen growth rates at OpenAI and Anthropic.

news.title: Red Lobster's Ultimate Endless Shrimp promotion described as a 'car crash' for the company, lawsuit says
news.summary: "Thai Union doubled down on a campaign to squeeze out every drop of value that it could," creditors said

news.title: Billionaire investor Jeremy Grantham says bitcoin will 'dwindle away with a whimper'
news.summary: Jeremy Grantham renewed his long-running criticism of bitcoin, predicting it will gradually fade into irrelevance over decades.

news.title: Why breakthrough GLP-1 weight loss pills may be a bad thing for employer insurance coverage
news.summary: New GLP-1 weight-loss pills from Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly are likely to boost consumer demand, but employer-sponsored health insurance may not foot the bill.

news.title: US Natural Gas Drops on Cooler Outlooks as July Contract Expires
news.summary: US natural gas futures settled much lower in the final day of trading for the July contract as midday forecasts shifted to show cooler weather compared with overnight outlooks. Individual transactions moved the market more amid a drop-off in liquidity near expiry.

news.title: The brands winning with World Cup advertising may not be the sponsors
news.summary: The World Cup winners and losers in advertising may be indicative of a broader trend of how people are responding to authentic branding.

news.title: One factor that may be partly behind the S&P 500's curious action this week
news.summary: There were plenty of reasons for stocks to post a big move on Thursday, but the S&P 500 ended almost exactly where it closed Wednesday.

news.title: Polymarket annualized revenue surpasses $1 billion six weeks after its U.S. exchange launch
news.summary: World Cup trading and the end of its U.S. waitlist are driving rapid growth for Polymarket's prediction market platform.

news.title: Rise in memory chip costs puts pressure on retailers of laptops and smartphones
news.summary: As the global race for AI moves forward, a shortage of memory chips has begun to drive up prices of consumer electronics and may lead to product shortages.

news.title: 'The cult of Elon': SpaceX investors grapple with volatility amid big swings
news.summary: SpaceX stock has seen big spikes and drop in its opening two weeks as a public company.

news.title: Volkswagen plans to cut 15% of its workforce and close four German plants, report says
news.summary: Auto giant Volkswagen is reportedly planning to cut 100,000 jobs and end production at four German plants over the coming years.

news.title: Estrogen patches are hard to find, and it may not be resolved any time soon
news.summary: Estrogen patches are in short supply as demand for menopause treatments skyrockets, and it could take at least a year for manufacturers to catch up.

news.title: US Chipmaker Selloff Pushes Key Stock Indexes to Weekly Loss
news.summary: US stocks ended the week lower as ongoing weakness in chip stocks outweighed a survey showing cooler-than-expected inflation expectations and easing rate hike concerns.

news.title: EM Stocks Post Worst Week in More Than Three Months on AI Rout
news.summary: Emerging-market stocks posted their biggest weekly decline since early March, weighed down by a renewed selloff in technology shares that hammered South Korean equities.

news.title: Gold Climbs Above $4,000 as Inflation Print Curbs Rate-Hike Bets
news.summary: Gold extended gains above $4,000 an ounce on Friday after the latest US inflation print tempered expectations for an interest-rate hike, capping a tumultuous week that saw bullion slump to the lowest price since November.
